failure_reason: Cited block p002_c0004 raw text ('41,000 weekday riders – 7th busiest bus route in city') does not explicitly mention 'B44' or 'SBS'. The relation to route_b44-sbs (service_variant: sbs) relies on document-level co-occurrence in a CB9 SBS presentation, not on block-level evidence. Per instructions, co-occurrence in a document is not a relation. Additionally, in September 2011 the B44 SBS was still proposed (launched Nov 2013), so the ridership metric describes the existing B44 Limited/Local service, not the SBS variant — yet the do_not_merge list explicitly separates route_b44-local from route_b44-sbs.

Same block (p002_c0004) as d01. Raw text 'Buses travel at an average speed of 7-8 mph' does not mention 'B44' or 'SBS'. Connection to route_b44-sbs relies on document co-occurrence, and the 2011 speed metric describes existing pre-SBS service on the corridor.
Same block (p002_c0004). Raw text '5,500 people board in CB9' does not mention 'B44' or 'SBS'. Connection to route_b44-sbs relies on document co-occurrence only.

Cited block p002_c0006 raw text ('300,000 residents • 62% of households do not own a car...') does not mention 'Nostrand Avenue', 'corridor', or 'within 1/4 mile'. The metric payload fills in scope from document context, but the block evidence alone does not state a connection to corridor_nostrand-avenue.
